title: In the End

earnings.assumptions: Estimate models very large ongoing streaming volume, gaming-era nostalgia, and durable rock-catalog playlist demand.

ownership.master_owner: Major-label master ownership with artist-side royalty participation
ownership.publishing_owner: Publishing appears split among band writers and publishers
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No song-specific sale adjustment is modeled
ownership.notes: Modeled annual range based on current streaming and legacy rock-catalog economics.

meaning_summary: In the End keeps earning because its piano figure, chorus, and emotional directness made it one of the most durable rock songs of the streaming era.
revenue_drivers: 2000s rock nostalgia and alternative playlists keep the song in heavy catalog circulation.
The chorus is immediately recognizable, which helps repeat listening and social reuse.
Band-wide catalog demand keeps Hybrid Theory tracks linked together economically.
